Code-strip release. No new features. Every item removes dead or near-dead code, narrows module visibility, or consolidates parallel FFI surfaces. `tdbe` bumps to `0.12.0` because the `errors` public module is removed (folded into `tdbe::error`).

See the [v8.0.7 entry in the structured changelog](https://github.com/userFRM/ThetaDataDx/blob/v8.0.7/CHANGELOG.md#807---2026-04-23) for the full detail.

## Removed

- The internal MDDS normalization forwarding layer. The wire canonicalizers move next to their generated consumers; no public surface change.
- A 90-line streaming reconnect function with zero callers. `ThetaDataDx::reconnect_streaming` remains the reconnect entry point.
- The crate-local right-parser re-export shim. `parse_right`, `parse_right_strict`, `ParsedRight` stay at the crate root via `tdbe::right::*`.
- An unreachable retry-classification trio and the crate-level `#![allow(dead_code)]` attribute that masked it.
- The deprecated `errors` module on `tdbe` — folded into `tdbe::error`. The two used items remain reachable at `tdbe::error::*`; the unused helper and the old module are gone.
- 24 `FpssClient` / `ThetaDataDx` per-security shortcut methods (and their unsubscribe twins). Callers use the `Contract`-taking `subscribe_quotes` / `subscribe_trades` / `subscribe_open_interest` methods directly.
- 61 `MddsClient::<endpoint>_with_deadline` sibling methods on every list endpoint. Per-call deadlines route through `EndpointArgs::with_timeout_ms` (FFI / Python / TS / Go / C++) or the builder `.with_deadline(Duration)` setter on parsed endpoints. SDK generators now apply the deadline locally instead of calling the deleted `_with_deadline` variant.
- 61 `tdx_<endpoint>` (no-options) FFI entry points. Every SDK — C++, Go, Python, TypeScript — already calls the `tdx_<endpoint>_with_options` variants, so the plain-name declarations in the C header and the hand-written historical FFI wrappers are gone.
- The protobuf-crate re-export at the `thetadatadx` crate root. Downstream consumers that need it now pull the protobuf crate in as a direct dependency pinned to the same version.
- `MddsClient::raw_query`, `MddsClient::raw_query_info`, `MddsClient::channel` — zero callers anywhere in the tree.

## Changed

- `pub mod unified` and `pub mod registry` narrowed to `pub(crate)`. The documented types (`ThetaDataDx`, `SubscriptionInfo`, `ConnectionStatus`, `EndpointMeta`, `ParamMeta`, `ParamType`, `ReturnType`, `ENDPOINTS`, plus `by_category`, `find`, `param_type_to_json_type`, `CATEGORIES` for the CLI / MCP tools) stay public via `pub use`.
- `DirectConfig::production_defaults` narrowed to crate-private; its only caller is in-crate.

## Changed

- Every Rust crate version bumps `8.0.6 → 8.0.7`: `thetadatadx`, `thetadatadx-ffi`, `thetadatadx-cli`, `thetadatadx-server`, `thetadatadx-mcp`, `thetadatadx-py`, `thetadatadx-napi`.
- `sdks/typescript/package.json` + every platform subpackage under `sdks/typescript/npm/` bumps to `8.0.7` so the npm dependency graph stays coherent.
- `tdbe` bumps `0.11.0 → 0.12.0` because the public `tdbe::error` module is removed. The used items stay reachable at `tdbe::error::*`.
- Five `Cargo.lock`s refreshed (workspace root + `sdks/python`, `sdks/typescript`, `tools/mcp`, `tools/server`).
- Verification: workspace formatting, clippy (default and with `polars,arrow`), and tests (default and with `polars,arrow`); the Python binding library tests; the byte-identical generator-regen check; and the generator drift check.
